title: Compose: Absolute path in Dockerfile crashes does when context is specified
body:
### Issue Description

When trying to generate a dev container in VS Code, I'm running into a podman issue, which doesn't happen with docker.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a simple `Dockerfile` in a new folder:

```text
FROM scratch
````

2. Create `docker-compose.yml` in the same folder and edit accordingly

```yml
version: '3'

services:
  app:
    image: bla
    build:
      dockerfile: Q:\Code\Container\EmptyDir\Dockerfile # Change path to your docker-compose.yml
      context: c:\Users\virzak\AppData\Roaming\Code\User\globalStorage\ms-vscode-remote.remote-containers\data\empty-folder # Use any empty folder if this doesn't exist
```

3. From that same folder run `podman compose build`. Compare to `docker compose build`


### Describe the results you received

Podman throws an error:

```
🚀  podman compose build
>>>> Executing external compose provider "C:\\Python313\\Scripts\\podman-compose.exe". Please see podman-compose(1) for how to disable this message. <<<<

Error: no Containerfile or Dockerfile specified or found in context directory, c:\Users\virzak\AppData\Roaming\Code\User\globalStorage\ms-vscode-remote.remote-containers\data\empty-folder: The system cannot find the file specified.
Error: executing C:\Python313\Scripts\podman-compose.exe build: exit status 125
```

### Describe the results you expected

Expected docker behaviour:

```
🚀  docker compose build
time="2025-12-30T08:17:45-05:00" level=warning msg="Q:\\Code\\Container\\EmptyDir\\docker-compose.yml: the attribute `version` is obsolete, it will be ignored, please remove it to avoid potential confusion"
[+] Building 1.0s (5/5) FINISHED
 => [internal] load local bake definitions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    0.0s
 => => reading from stdin 582B                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                0.0s
 => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          0.1s
 => => transferring dockerfile: 51B                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           0.0s
 => [internal] load .dockerignore                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             0.0s
 => => transferring context: 2B                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               0.0s
 => exporting to image                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        0.1s
 => => writing image sha256:7a6b996e51dc8ee7dd49a587888561a562daf468c6f05f15ecc850ba8dfb5868                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  0.0s
 => => naming to docker.io/library/bla                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        0.0s
 => resolving provenance for metadata file                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    0.0s
[+] Building 1/1
 ✔ bla  Built        
```

### podman info output


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Podman works fine when `context` is omitted in `docker-compose.yml`

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Does this replicate if you use Docker Compose instead of podman-compose? We don't support the podman-compose project.

I installed docker-compose and docker-cli using choco and ran. The same result:

```
🚀  docker compose build
time="2026-01-01T00:44:55-05:00" level=warning msg="Q:\\Code\\Container\\EmptyDir\\docker-compose.yml: the attribute `version` is obsolete, it will be ignored, please remove it to avoid potential confusion"
time="2026-01-01T00:44:55-05:00" level=warning msg="Docker Compose requires buildx plugin to be installed"
[+] build 0/1
 - Image bla Building                                                                                              0.0s
unable to prepare context: unable to get relative Dockerfile path: Rel: can't make Q:\Code\Container\EmptyDir\Dockerfile relative to C:\Users\virzak\AppData\Roaming\Code\User\globalStorage\ms-vscode-remote.remote-containers\data\empty-folder
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Have the same issue, but does not even require an absolute path to the Dockerfile. Compose was also created by VS Code Dev Containers, but I get the same issue when trying to build directly:
docker-compose.yaml & Dockerfile

```yaml version: '3.8' services: app: build: context: . dockerfile: Dockerfile volumes: - ../..:/workspaces:cached # Overrides default command so things don't shut down after the process ends. command: sleep infinity # Runs app on the same network as the database container, allows "forwardPorts" in devcontainer.json function. network_mode: service:db # Use "forwardPorts" in **devcontainer.json** to forward an app port locally. # (Adding the "ports" property to this file will not forward from a Codespace.) db: image: mariadb:10.4 restart: unless-stopped volumes: - mariadb-data:/var/lib/mysql environment: MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: mariadb MYSQL_DATABASE: mariadb MYSQL_USER: mariadb MYSQL_PASSWORD: mariadb # Add "forwardPorts": ["3306"] to **devcontainer.json** to forward MariaDB locally. # (Adding the "ports" property to this file will not forward from a Codespace.) volumes: mariadb-data: ``` ```Dockerfile FROM mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/php:3-8.5-trixie # Install MariaDB client RUN apt-get update && export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \ && apt-get install -y mariadb-client \ && apt-get clean -y && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* # Install php-mysql driver RUN docker-php-ext-install mysqli pdo pdo_mysql # [Optional] Uncomment this section to install additional OS packages. # RUN apt-get update && export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \ # && apt-get -y install --no-install-recommends # [Optional] Uncomment this line to install global node packages. # RUN su vscode -c "source /usr/local/share/nvm/nvm.sh && npm install -g " 2>&1 ```

Same error with `docker-compose` & `podman compose` (which seems only to be wrapper for the former, according to Podman Desktop & because `podman-compose` is not installed on my device): ``` PS > docker-compose build time="2026-04-01T14:59:44+02:00" level=warning msg="C:\\Users\\of4710\\projects\\Windows\\windows-reports\\.devcontainer\\docker-compose.yml: the attribute `version` is obsolete, it will be ignored, please remove it to avoid potential confusion" time="2026-04-01T14:59:44+02:00" level=warning msg="Docker Compose requires buildx plugin to be installed" [+] build 0/1 - Image devcontainer-app Building 0.0s unable to prepare context: unable to evaluate symlinks in context path: The system cannot find the path specified. ```
